title: HEAT INDEX WATCH: 44°C logged in 4 areas on Thursday, May 7 - Inquirer.net
search_term: (heatstroke OR "heat stroke" OR sunstroke OR "heat exhaustion" OR "heat-related death" OR "heat casualty" OR "heat fatality" OR "suspected heat death" OR dehydration OR "heat cramps" OR "heat illness") Bihar
full_text:
MANILA, Philippines — Heat indices recorded on Thursday, May 7, peaked at 44ºC in Pangasinan, Ilocos Norte, Cagayan and Occidental Mindoro, the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa) said.
Heat index refers to the heat the human body feels when air temperature and humidity are combined.
Once it ranges between 42ºC and 51ºC, Pagasa tags the index as “danger” level amid increased risks of heat cramps and heat exhaustion, which could then lead to heat stroke.
Based on data released by Pagasa, Dagupan City, Pangasinan; MMSU, Batac, Ilocos Norte; Aparri, Cagayan; and San Jose, Occidental Mindoro each recorded a peak of 44ºC on Thursday.
Other areas that recorded danger-level heat indices across the country the same day are as follows:
Sinait, Ilocos Sur – 43ºC
Laoag City, Ilocos Norte – 43ºC
Bacnotan, La Union – 42ºC
Tuguegarao City, Cagayan – 43ºC
Baler (Radar), Aurora – 42ºC
Sangley Point, Cavite City, Cavite – 42ºC
Coron, Palawan – 42ºC
Puerto Princesa, Palawan – 43ºC
Cuyo, Palawan – 43ºC
Daet, Camarines Norte – 42ºC
CBSUA-Pili, Camarines Sur – 42ºC
Roxas City, Capiz – 42ºC
Iloilo City, Iloilo – 42ºC
Dumangas, Iloilo – 43ºC
Catbalogan, Western Samar – 42ºC
Borongan, Eastern Samar – 43ºC /das

title: 22 areas forecast to hit ‘danger’ level heat indices on Friday, May 8 - Inquirer.net
search_term: (heatstroke OR "heat stroke" OR sunstroke OR "heat exhaustion" OR "heat-related death" OR "heat casualty" OR "heat fatality" OR "suspected heat death" OR dehydration OR "heat cramps" OR "heat illness") Bihar
full_text:
22 areas forecast to hit ‘danger’ level heat indices on Friday, May 8
MANILA, Philippines — Twenty-two areas are forecast to hit danger-level heat indices on Friday, May 8, said the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical, and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa).
Heat index, which refers to the heat the human body feels when air temperature and humidity are combined, is automatically categorized as “danger” level once it ranges between 42°C and 51°C.
Pagasa said indices under this level carry increased risks of heat cramps and heat exhaustion increases which could then lead to heat stroke.
Based on Pagasa’s forecast as of 5 p.m. of Thursday, May 7, heat indices across the country are forecast to peak in Aparri, Cagayan at 45°C.
Meanwhile, below are the other areas expected hit danger level indices on the same day:
- Borongan, Eastern Samar – 43ºC /mr
